moving_task_templates.title: Meet with Financial Advisor

moving_task_templates.title: Sort and Downsize Belongings
moving_task_templates.description: Decide what to keep, donate, or give to family before packing.

moving_task_templates.description: Box up seasonal clothing, décor, and rarely used items.

moving_task_templates.title: Schedule Utility Transfers
moving_task_templates.description: Arrange start and stop dates for electricity, internet, and cable.

moving_task_templates.title: Confirm Utilities for Move-in Day
moving_task_templates.description: Verify that power, water, and internet will be active on your move-in date.

moving_task_templates.title: Submit Change of Address with Post Office
moving_task_templates.description: File your address change online or at the post office so mail forwards correctly.

moving_task_templates.title: Update Address for Subscriptions & Deliveries
moving_task_templates.description: Update magazines, meal kits, and regular deliveries with your new condo address.

moving_task_templates.title: Transfer Medical Records
moving_task_templates.description: Arrange for your medical records to be shared with new nearby providers if needed.

moving_task_templates.title: Update Preferred Pharmacy
moving_task_templates.description: Select a nearby pharmacy and transfer prescriptions.

moving_task_templates.title: Gather Important Legal Documents
moving_task_templates.description: Collect wills, powers of attorney, insurance policies, and IDs in one secure folder.

moving_task_templates.title: Set Up Secure Document Storage
moving_task_templates.description: Decide where important documents and spare keys will be stored in your new home.

moving_task_templates.title: Introduce Yourself to Neighbors
moving_task_templates.description: Plan a friendly hello to neighbors and building staff during your first week.

moving_task_templates.title: Schedule Pre-Move Walkthrough
moving_task_templates.description: Walk through your condo with staff to review finishes, appliances, and access.

policy_documents.title: Pet Policy and Small Dog Guidelines
policy_documents.content:
Evergreen Heights is a pet-friendly 55+ community. Residents may keep up to two small pets per condominium, with a combined maximum of two cats or dogs. Small dogs are defined as dogs that weigh 25 pounds or less at full adult weight. Dogs around 20 pounds are generally approved as long as they are well-trained and meet temperament guidelines.

Key rules for small dogs:
- Maximum weight: 25 lbs at full adult size (verification may be requested from a veterinarian).
- Maximum of two pets per home, including dogs and cats.
- All dogs must be house-trained, current on vaccinations, and licensed according to local regulations.
- Leashes are required in all common areas, and pets are not permitted in the main dining room or indoor pool area.
- Persistent barking, aggressive behavior, or damage to common areas may result in revocation of pet privileges.

Approval Process:
- Residents must submit a Pet Approval Request form to the HOA before bringing a new dog into the community.
- The request should include the dog’s age, breed or mix, approximate weight (e.g., 20 lbs), and proof of vaccinations.
- Written approval must be received from the HOA prior to move-in of the pet.

Dogs expected to exceed 40 pounds at full adult weight are not permitted. Exceptions for service animals and support animals are reviewed on a case-by-case basis in accordance with applicable laws.

policy_documents.title: Guest Suite Reservation & Use Policy
policy_documents.content:
Guest suites are available for short-term stays by family and friends of current residents. All reservations are processed on a first-come, first-served basis and require a completed Guest Suite Request form.

Reservation Guidelines:
- Minimum stay: 1–2 nights depending on suite type; maximum stay typically 7–14 nights.
- A confirmed resident sponsor is required for each reservation.
- Nightly rates vary by suite size and location; current rates are listed on the Guest Suites page.
- Accessible and near-elevator suites are prioritized for guests with mobility needs.

Payment & Check-In:
- Payment is due at the time of confirmation and may be charged to the resident’s account.
- Check-in time is 3:00 PM; check-out time is 11:00 AM unless otherwise arranged.
- Guests must follow all community rules, including quiet hours and non-smoking policies.

Cancellations made more than 72 hours before arrival receive a full refund. Cancellations within 72 hours may incur a fee of one night’s stay.

policy_documents.title: Community Conduct & Quiet Hours
policy_documents.content:
To support a calm and respectful living environment, Evergreen Heights observes community-wide quiet hours and expectations for conduct.

Quiet Hours:
- Daily quiet hours are from 10:00 PM to 7:00 AM.
- During quiet hours, residents and guests should keep noise from televisions, music, and conversations at a level that is not audible in neighboring units.

General Conduct:
- Courteous behavior is expected in all common areas, including the lobby, dining room, fitness center, and library.
- Harassment, discrimination, or disruptive behavior is not tolerated and may result in HOA action.
- Smoking and vaping are prohibited inside buildings and only permitted in designated outdoor areas.

Community rooms may be reserved for private gatherings with prior approval, provided that events conclude by 9:30 PM and respect noise guidelines.

policy_documents.title: Parking, Garages, and Mobility Devices
policy_documents.content:
Parking at Evergreen Heights is assigned and managed by the HOA to ensure fair access and safety.

Resident Parking:
- Each condominium is assigned at least one parking space, either in the parking garage or surface lot.
- Additional spaces may be available for a monthly fee when inventory allows.
- Vehicles must display the community parking permit at all times.

Guest Parking:
- Short-term guest parking is available in clearly marked visitor spaces.
- Guests staying in guest suites should register their vehicle at the front desk upon arrival.

Mobility Devices:
- Motorized scooters and wheelchairs may be parked in designated storage areas or within the resident’s unit.
- Charging of mobility devices must comply with posted safety guidelines and not block hallways or exits.

policy_documents.title: HOA Governance & Dues Overview
policy_documents.content:
Evergreen Heights is governed by a homeowners association (HOA) elected by the resident-owners. The HOA board oversees community finances, maintenance, and policy enforcement.

Dues & Budget:
- Monthly HOA dues cover building maintenance, common utilities, amenities, reserves, and basic services.
- A summary of the annual operating budget is distributed to all owners each year.

Board & Meetings:
- The HOA board consists of resident volunteers elected to staggered terms.
- Regular board meetings are held at least quarterly and are open to residents, with time reserved for owner comments.

Policy Changes:
- Proposed changes to major policies, such as the pet policy or guest suite rules, are shared with residents for feedback prior to adoption whenever feasible.
- Final policy documents and updates are posted on the Resident Resources & Policies page.

pet_policy_rules.description: Small dogs (up to 25 lbs at full adult weight) are generally approved, subject to temperament and behavior guidelines. A well-trained 20-pound dog is considered a typical small dog under this policy.
Medium dogs between 26 and 40 lbs may be considered on a case-by-case basis. Additional documentation from a veterinarian and a behavior review may be required.
Large dogs expected to exceed 40 lbs at full adult weight are not permitted under the standard pet policy, with the exception of qualifying service or support animals reviewed individually.
Indoor cats up to approximately 20 lbs are permitted, with a maximum of two pets per home including dogs and cats. Cats must be spayed or neutered and kept indoors at all times.
Small caged animals such as birds, fish, and certain small mammals may be allowed with prior HOA approval. Larger exotic pets are not permitted.
